name: Wild Hunt
description: The Wild Hunt is a motif prominent in the folklore of Germanic-speaking countries of Europe and beyond. It typically involves a ghostly or spectral group of hunters, often led by a mythological figure such as Odin (Wodan), Frau Holle, or other local deities or legendary figures. The Hunt is usually depicted as a chaotic, noisy procession sweeping across the sky or along the earth, often at night or during storms. Its appearance is considered an ill omen, portending war, plague, or death. Those who witness the Hunt may be swept away and forced to join, or suffer other misfortunes. The exact details of the Hunt vary significantly from region to region, but the core elements of a group of spectral hunters and hounds, a fearsome leader, and the association with death and misfortune remain consistent. The Wild Hunt served as a way to explain unexplained noises in the night, natural disasters, and periods of societal upheaval, instilling both fear and respect for the power of nature and the supernatural.
subtitle: Spectral hunters of the storm.
appearance: The appearance of the Wild Hunt varies. Sometimes the hunters are seen as ghostly figures clad in armor, riding skeletal horses, accompanied by packs of spectral hounds with glowing eyes. Other accounts depict them as monstrous beings, or even as deceased souls condemned to eternally hunt. The leader is often described as a tall, imposing figure, cloaked and hooded, with wild eyes and a fierce demeanor. The overall impression is one of dread and otherworldly power.
habitat: The Wild Hunt is typically said to roam across the sky, through forests, or over fields, primarily during the winter months or during storms. They are often associated with liminal spaces, such as the boundaries between worlds, or during times of transition, such as Samhain or Yule.
behavior: The Wild Hunt is generally considered to be malevolent or at least dangerous. They are often seen as harbingers of misfortune, and those who encounter them are at risk of being swept away, killed, or driven mad. In some accounts, they may offer a twisted form of assistance or guidance, but at a great cost. Their behavior is unpredictable and driven by forces beyond human comprehension.

keyMyths.title: Odin's Hunt
keyMyths.description: Odin, as the leader of the Wild Hunt, rides Sleipnir, his eight-legged horse, leading a host of fallen warriors across the sky. This hunt is a sign of impending war and chaos.

keyMyths.title: Frau Holle's Ride
keyMyths.description: In some regions, Frau Holle, a goddess associated with winter and spinning, leads the Hunt. She is often depicted as a benevolent figure, but her Hunt can still be dangerous to those who cross her path.

keyMyths.title: The Perchtenlauf
keyMyths.description: In Alpine regions, the Perchtenlauf involves masked figures representing the Wild Hunt parading through villages to drive away evil spirits and ensure a good harvest. These figures are often noisy and frightening.

sources.author: Jacob Grimm
sources.description: A comprehensive study of Germanic mythology, including detailed descriptions of the Wild Hunt.
sources.text: A comprehensive study of Germanic mythology, including detailed descriptions of the Wild Hunt.

sources.author: Claude Lecouteux
sources.description: Explores the connections between witchcraft, folklore, and the Wild Hunt.
sources.text: Explores the connections between witchcraft, folklore, and the Wild Hunt.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: Numerous collections of local legends and folktales that feature variations of the Wild Hunt.
sources.text: Numerous collections of local legends and folktales that feature variations of the Wild Hunt.
